probes.name: product_search_polish_availability
probes.prompt: Can you search for good induction cooktops with built-in extraction fans (downdraft), the kind that vent from the surface itself so you don't need a separate range hood. Start with reviews on YouTube. I'm interested in models available in Europe. After that check if these are actually available to buy in Poland and what the prices look like. Just send me the summary here with links and prices.
probes.criteria:
Agent is an internet research assistant that searches via Google and optionally deep-reads pages. A good response: (1) actually searches for induction cooktops with downdraft extraction, (2) names specific models/brands found in reviews, (3) includes links or references to sources, (4) checks Polish availability (Polish shops like Media Expert, Euro RTV AGD, Allegro, etc.) with approximate PLN prices, (5) writes in a conversational Telegram-friendly tone.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by real message 2 (rice cooker research with YouTube start and Polish price check).

probes.name: competition_deadlines_2026_check
probes.prompt: Can you methodically check if there is a 2026 edition of the International Mathematical Olympiad for university students — specifically the IMC (International Mathematics Competition for University Students) and also any Polish national-level math competitions for university students. I need to know: are submissions open, what are the exact deadlines, and can submissions be in Polish or must they be in English. Send me what you find with links to official pages.
probes.criteria:
Agent is an internet research assistant that searches via Google and deep-reads pages for specifics. probes.name: youtube_video_research
probes.prompt: Hey, can you search YouTube for recent videos (2025 or 2026) about using LLM agents for automated penetration testing and security auditing? I want to know what tools and frameworks people are demonstrating. Just give me a summary of the most relevant ones you find with video titles and channel names.
probes.criteria:
Agent is an internet research assistant that searches the web. A good response: (1) searches YouTube or the web for relevant videos on LLM agents in pentesting/security auditing, (2) lists at least 3-5 specific video results with titles and channel names, (3) briefly summarizes what each covers (tools, frameworks), (4) includes URLs or links, (5) stays conversational and does not hallucinate video titles that don't exist.

probes.name: deep_read_technical_comparison
probes.prompt: I need you to search for and compare two things: OpenHands (formerly OpenDevin) vs SWE-agent as AI software engineering agents. Specifically I want to know what benchmarks they report, what their architecture looks like, and which one seems more actively maintained right now in 2026. Deep-read their GitHub repos and any blog posts you find. Summarize the key differences and give me links to everything.
probes.criteria:
Agent is an internet research assistant that searches and optionally deep-reads pages. A good response: (1) searches for both OpenHands and SWE-agent, (2) visits GitHub repos and/or blog posts to extract concrete details (benchmark scores, architecture descriptions, commit activity), (3) presents a structured comparison, (4) includes direct links to sources, (5) notes which appears more actively maintained with evidence (recent commits, stars, release dates), (6) does not fabricate benchmark numbers.
